analysis.reasoning: Core message: Celebrates U.S. federal leadership and success in accelerating COVID-19 vaccination—enough supply for all adults by end of May, over 2 million shots per day, 95 million doses administered. Evaluation by narrative: US1 (honorable tradition) – Not referenced; no appeal to historical legacy or enduring values. US2 (admirable society) – Not about societal groups or civil society; focuses on government performance. US3 (hostile enemies) – No adversary mentioned. US4 (force for good) – While public health progress benefits people, the tweet is domestic, operational, and does not frame actions as promoting human rights or aiding oppressed peoples; not applicable. US5 (great international ally) – No allies or partnerships mentioned. US6 (at the forefront) – Implicitly positions the U.S. as leading/performing strongly in vaccine rollout with large numbers and ambitious timelines; this supports a leadership/forefront narrative. Conclusion: Only US6 is promoted.
